div1.entryFree:
Opposite, adj. 1) adverse, repugnant, hostile: free from a stubborn o. intent, H6B III, 2, 251. at their births good stars were o. R3 IV, 4, 215. be o. all planets of good luck to my proceedings, R3 IV, 4, 215 he's o. to humanity, Tim. I, 1, 284. how o. I stood to his purpose, Lr. II, 1, 51. so o. to marriage, Oth. I, 2, 67. Followed by with: be o. with a kinsman, Tw. II, 5, 162 (show him your aversion). to be thus o. with heaven, R3 II, 2, 94.
2) contrary: cross him with their o. persuasion, Lucr. 286. what is o. to England's love, John III, 1, 254. thou art as o. to every good as the Antipodes are unto us, H6C I, 4, 134. the office o. to Saint Peter, Oth. IV, 2, 91.
